seoTitle: Oyster Creek vs Pacific Haven — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Oyster Creek and Pacific Haven. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Oyster Creek vs Pacific Haven: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Oyster Creek and Pacific Haven.
heading: Oyster Creek vs Pacific Haven — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Oyster Creek, Queensland and Pacific Haven, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Oyster Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Pacific Haven (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Oyster Creek or Pacific Haven safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Oyster Creek has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Pacific Haven scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Oyster Creek scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Pacific Haven scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Oyster Creek or Pacific Haven?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Oyster Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
